title: Design System: Yu-Gi-Oh! Deck Builder & Collection

display.purpose: Card-name spotlight and page-title moments. Fixed rem, not fluid.

title.purpose: Card titles in tiles, dialog titles.

label.purpose: UI labels, chips, metadata. Sentence case.

data.purpose: Game data only: ATK/DEF, levels, passcodes, counts.

shadows.name: focus-glow
shadows.purpose: Standard :focus-visible ring: a void gap then a cobalt ring.

shadows.name: lift-hover
shadows.purpose: Soft diffuse lift under a card tile on hover; never for resting elevation.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default exponential ease-out for state transitions and card hover.

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Default transition duration (range 150-250ms). No page-load choreography.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single cobalt call-to-action: add to deck, save, confirm.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Neutral action on a surface: cancel, back, less-prominent choices.

components.name: Search Field
components.description: The search-forward entry point to card discovery.

components.name: Attribute Filter Chip
components.description: A filter pill that pairs color with an icon + label, so selection never relies on color alone.
components.html: <button class="ds-chip" aria-pressed="true"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M20 6 9 17l-5-5"></path></svg>DARK</button>

components.name: Card Tile
components.description: Signature component. Art-forward tile; chrome recedes, the card image leads. Image placeholder shown (do not hotlink ygoprodeck images; serve cached art).
components.html:
Dark Magician

ATK 2500DEF 2100

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Quiet top-bar navigation; current route marked with ink + a cobalt underline.
components.html: BrowseDecksCollection

narrative.overview: A dark, quiet room where each card is lit like an exhibit. The interface is the gallery wall and the track lighting, never the painting. Surfaces are a true, untinted near-black so the warm, saturated card art is the only real color in the room; the cobalt-indigo brand color glows in like a calibration light, used sparingly to mark what's live, selected, or actionable. The mood borrows from museum/gallery sites and premium dark product UI (Linear, Vercel, MUBI): confident, precise, unhurried. This system explicitly rejects the generic SaaS dashboard, the database-dump fan-site look, and the obvious category reflexes (all-gold card-trim chrome, mystical-purple everything).
keyCharacteristics: True untinted near-black surfaces (oklch(0.11 0 0)); the art carries color.
A single cobalt-indigo brand voice, used on ≤10% of any screen.
Depth from tonal layering + a faint colored glow, not heavy drop shadows.
One refined sans (Geist) across the whole UI; mono reserved for game data.
Responsive, state-conveying motion (150-250ms); no page-load choreography.
Information is never carried by color alone (YGO attribute/type coding).

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Calibration Cobalt appears on ≤10% of any given screen. Its rarity is what makes selected and primary legible at a glance.

rules.name: The Untinted Void Rule
rules.body: The background is pure neutral (chroma 0). Warmth and color come from the card art, never from a tinted surface.

rules.name: The No-Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: No tiny uppercase tracked kicker above sections. Labels are sentence case; hierarchy comes from size and weight.

rules.name: The Mono-Is-For-Machines Rule
rules.body: Geist Mono is reserved for game data and identifiers. It never sets prose, headings, or button labels.

rules.name: The Glow-Not-Drop Rule
rules.body: Convey depth with tonal layering and a faint colored glow. Drop shadows darker than oklch(0 0 0 / 0.5) or used for resting elevation are prohibited.

dos: Do keep the background a true untinted near-black (oklch(0.11 0 0)); let the card art be the color.
Do keep Calibration Cobalt to ≤10% of any screen (the One Voice Rule).
Do convey depth with tonal layering + a faint glow (the Glow-Not-Drop Rule).
Do pair every attribute/type/state color with an icon, label, or text - color is never the only signal.
Do use Geist Mono only for game data (ATK/DEF, levels, passcodes), Geist Sans for everything else.
Do give every interactive component default / hover / focus-visible / active / disabled / loading states, with a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
Do use skeletons for loading card grids and teaching empty states, not mid-content spinners.
donts: Don't build the generic SaaS dashboard: no cream/navy template, no hero-metric stat cards, no walls of identical icon+heading+text cards.
Don't add tracked-uppercase eyebrows or 01 / 02 / 03 numbered markers above sections.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text), side-stripe border-left/right accents, or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't tint the background warm or cool for mood; warmth lives in the art and accents.
Don't use drop shadows for resting elevation or anything darker than oklch(0 0 0 / 0.5).
Don't set prose, headings, or button labels in mono; don't set UI labels in a display face.
Don't rely on attribute/type color alone to convey meaning anywhere.
